candidates.stmt: Brentford is already over-developed and enough is enough.  Our local services, including GP surgeries, are just not up to coping with all of this.  We cannot continue using endless amount of concrete and steel if we are to meet our climate commitments. We need to look at other options, including the refurbishment of the Heidelberg building and the north of the High Street.

Guy started working in Brentford in 1977, and made it his home in 2008. Now retired, Guy serves as the Chair of the local credit union, and a trustee of Hounslow Community Foodbox. He already has a strong track record of supporting local residents, from averting evictions and having people in need rehoused, to clearing hundreds of instances of flytipping and grafitti.

Guy is passionate about social justice, and wants to work as a Councillor to defend ward residents. He hopes to shape the council in ensuring it delivers efficient, economic services, particularly to the most disadvantaged residents. He hopes to have regular interaction and dialogue with residents, and also communicate the work of the council through his widely-read blog.

Previously he was the CEO of a non-profit company, and a board director of a large IT company. He is keen to offer the skills and knowledge developed by this work to the council in whatever ways he can, particularly supporting the work of planning and scrutiny, licensing, audit, and pension committees.

I want to be a strong Labour voice for our town on the Council. Brentford is a place to be proud of, and our community spirit is one of the reasons I made Brentford my home.

I have lived the pressures many face finding an affordable home or keeping up with the cost of living, and those experiences have shaped my commitment to community action as a school governor, food bank volunteer, youth mentor, and campaigner with Ruth Cadbury MP.

I believe in fairness, opportunity, and community, and that Brentford’s best days lie ahead. I want a town where young people can thrive, homes are genuinely affordable, and older residents are valued and cared for. We all deserve safe, clean, green, and vibrant streets, and councillors who listen and act on your priorities.

I’ll always put you, your family and our town first - fighting for a fairer, brighter Brentford.

Joe Bourke has lived and worked in Brentford for 30 years. As a Chartered Certified Accountant, he is Head of Subject for Accounting and Finance at the University of West London, Brentford Campus. He currently serves as a voluntary treasurer for the Musical Museum Charity in Brentford High Street and is a longstanding supporter of Brentford Football Club.

1/ Brentford needs real people on the council to stick up for forgotten Brentford.
2/Every decision I make as an independent will be for Brentford and not for a party whip! 
3/ I want Brentford’s high street to thrive not just survive. 
4/ I will chase up and continue to ask questions about our arts centre. 
5/ I will make sure development monies from Brentford go to Brentford !

Gary has been a resident of what is now Brentford West for the last 24 years, working as a Product Manager for a software company. His children went to a local primary school, where he became the chair of Governors, as he is a passionate believer in the power of community. As a councillor he wants to preserve that community, making streets safer, cleaner, and protect them from being dominated by high-rise poor-quality housing.

I moved into Chiswick (to a flat on the Turnham Green ward side of Devonshire Road) in 1983 and currently live in the Gunnersbury Park Garden Estate conservation area in the north west corner of the ward. I spend my life reporting fly-tipping and overflowing bins, campaigning against speeding and rat runs and lobbying for improved street lighting, safer road junctions and better pedestrian crossings. I’m obsessed with our independent shops, cafés and restaurants, supporting them as much as is humanly possible; they are, for me, what makes Chiswick special. I can often be found in a local café with a cup of coffee and, if it’s brunch time, avocado smash (with halloumi, a poached egg and dukkah). I want to be a strong voice for Chiswickians, protecting our lifestyle and keeping our area special. In my other life, I’m a self-employed reputation crisis management consultant having previously worked in politics, lobbying and corporate communications and as an occasional journalist.

Guy is a retiree and has lived in Chiswick for 12 years. He ran a recycling paper and glass group in the mid-70s and is still keen on minimising carbon footprint. He believes that everybody has the weight to vote for those who best reflect their views and that the Liberal Democrats have the right attitude to holding this council to account for its actions.

Jon Elkon taught at Featherstone High, a multicultural school in a very deprived area.  His commitment to his pupils was rewarded with several awards including the prestigious Pearson’s Teaching Award. He has been Union Representative, representing the teachers in disputes with management and looking after their interests. He is a published author of satirical fiction and poetry and runs a local self-defence and Karate Club.

Jon’s involvement in politics began with work for Anti-Apartheid, fighting racism and discrimination. Nowadays, his commitment is to protect and improve the environment.   Jon said “Local politics needs green voices. The local environment needs guardians who are not in the pockets of business or vested interests.”

Paul moved to Grove Park with his wife and young family almost twenty years ago. Their twin daughters grew up here and were educated locally - this has very much been the family home. With the twins now graduated and making their own way in the world, Paul has more time to indulge his love of paddling on the river. A senior technology executive who has spent his career building and scaling new businesses, he is standing because he believes Gunnersbury deserves a strong local voice.

Nik’s family has been in Chiswick for nearly four decades, and he moved back to Gunnersbury 19 years ago shortly after his first child was born. He now lives with his wife, three teenagers, a grown-up nephew, his stepmother in her eighties and two dogs, giving him first-hand experience of the wide range of local interests and community services in the area. Ten years ago he helped grow the 118-118 business from his bedroom to becoming CEO of five European companies, and now works as a consultant, regularly using the Tube, buses and walking to meetings, which keeps him aware of the importance of strong public transport links and thriving local businesses.

Michael is a former Hounslow councillor who grew up in Chiswick and has lived in the borough all his life. He studied History at York University and went on to study law. After qualifying as a solicitor he now works in the energy sector. He has been involved in local charities like The Shelter Project Hounslow and local environment initiatives such as Thames Strategy: Kew to Chelsea. As a councillor Michael was an active member of Hounslow’s Planning Committee and played a key role in reforming the Council's constitution.

He'll champion transparency and value for money in the council and at the Chiswick Area Forum. He will seek greater value for residents from the council, such as more effective leaf sweeping and greater action on potholes. He will fight for a greater proportion of affordable and accessible housing in future developments while defending the historical beauty of Chiswick. He will work with residents and companies to prioritise the cleanliness of our river, for river users, and aim to improve the Council’s cleaning and recycling rates. He will push for a review of local street lighting, and a review of unsafe cycle lanes.

I am a law graduate from the SOAS, University of London, and have lived in Hounslow and Chiswick for over 14 years. I’ve been actively involved in local and national politics, including working for a local MP and supporting Labour’s General Election campaign.
Through my work as a caseworker and in the community, I understand the issues residents face and have built strong local relationships with community leaders and businesses.
If elected, I will serve the ward and focus on tackling crime and anti-social behaviour, improving street cleaning, ensuring adequate community services, and expanding youth opportunities and community projects.

As a councillor for the past eight years in the London Borough of Hounslow, I have supported residents on a wide range of local issues, including tackling crime,  fly-tipping, helping to keep our borough clean and green. I have also worked closely with and supported local food banks, recognising the importance of community support networks.

Over the years, I have built strong relationships with residents across the borough, particularly in Chiswick. Through consistent engagement, including regular door knocking, I have taken the time to listen to residents’ concerns and understand the local issues.

Alongside my role as a councillor, I work as an engineer in Chiswick Business Park. This has given me the opportunity to regularly interact with local residents on the high street, further strengthening my connection with  the local community.

Maya has lived in Chiswick for most of her life. Having grown up in area she feels she has utilised most of what Chiswick has to offer from its local schools, to dog walks in Chiswick House and eating at the many restaurants along the High Road.

If elected she will do everything in her power to improve green spaces and biodiversity, accessibility and local services such as street cleaning, whilst attempting to tackle crime at its core by trying to better youth and mental health services. As someone with a disability, Maya believes strongly in supporting those most vulnerable within the community. As a councillor she would offer fresh perspectives, hold the council accountable and be transparent with residents on the Council's plans.

Helen has lived in Chiswick for over 20 years, she is a businesswoman who has built a successful career in AI and Robotics. As a councillor she will fight for: Ensuring there is a voice for positive action representing Chiswick on the council, Reducing pollution and traffic in Chiswick to make it a healthier place to live and Better investment in Chiswick to improve services and access for all groups including the young, the elderly and those with disabilities

Leigh has lived in Chiswick for fifteen years, he is a chartered accountant. Along with many others, he was dismayed and appalled by the intolerance of the rhetoric in the lead up to the EU referendum. Leigh is committed to an open, tolerant and fair society, investing in public services and ensuring everyone is given the opportunities to meet their potential, and to live their lives free of fear, prejudice or discrimination.

As a councillor he will work hard for the people of Chiswick to:

    Better hold the council to account on planning and developments to ensure new projects enhance Chiswick by reducing pollution, improving infrastructure, increasing accessibility for pedestrians, cyclists and users of public transport, and introducing more green spaces
    Fight for investment in essential public services including real community policing and support for local hospitals
    Fully oppose any expansion or third runway at Heathrow

Nationally he will campaign for the UK to remain in the EU as a leading and influential member, and to keep Britain a liberal, democratic, and socially just society.

Arthur moved into the area over 20 years ago as his work in IT brought him closer to London. Now retired, he has more time for his children and grandchildren, all living not too far away, which often means helping with childcare for working parents. It also means he is more able to support his football team, AFC Wimbledon, watch more cricket, and finally have time to maintain his allotment.

"It is an immense honour to represent this beautiful ward on the Council. If re-elected, I'll continue to be a champion of positive politics, ensuring that the Councilworks with residents to secure the very best outcomes for all."

I have lived in the borough for 20 years, raising my two sons through excellent state schools. Trained as a barrister, I have worked in Higher Education for 24 years and been a school governor for nearly a decade.

As a Chiswick Riverside councillor,I have delivered on resurfacing, fly-tipping, anti-social behaviour and support for older residents.

I am a trustee of Chiswick House and Gardens Trust and Chiswick Pier Trust, and will be the President of the Chiswick Horticultural and Allotment Society in May 2026.

Please visit www.chiswick-labour.co.uk to learn more about how I and the other members of the Labour team will keep Chiswick beautiful.

Gabriella currently works as a Project Manager for an International Medical and Travel Security company based in Chiswick Park, having joined the company in April 2012.  Prior to starting her current role, she was based in Aberdeen for 2 and a half years, where she was responsible for setting up sales business practices for the company across the North Sea, regularly travelling to Norway to speak to clients and deliver training.

Outside of work, Gabriella has acted as a trustee of a charity which raises awareness and funds for causes in Central America, and has also worked to get young people involved and engaged in politics. Most recently, her focus has been concentrated on projects looking at equality in the workplace and supporting aspiring business women.

Gabriella is also an avid cyclist, not just for those short trips around Chiswick, but also taking part in endurance rides. She has helped to raise over £5000 for London’s Air Ambulance by cycling from London to Paris, and completing the London-Surrey 100 twice. She has agreed to cycle this route again in 2018, once more to raise funds for London’s Air Ambulance and is planning to cycle from London to Amsterdam later in the year.

As such, she sees the need to improve safer cycling in the borough on all routes, seeing that this can be done in a way that impacts residents who choose to cycle on smaller roads more than main ones.

Rick Rowe brings a strong record of public service, leadership and delivery. He has held senior policy and commercial roles in public transport, most recently serving as Head of Policy – New Services at Transport for London. His background spans transport, regulation, procurement, commercial strategy and public accountability.

A Chiswick Riverside resident, Rick is campaigning for cleaner air, safer streets, better local services and councillors who are visible, responsive and rooted in the community.

Rick Rowe said:
“Local people deserve councillors who know the area, listen properly and are prepared to stand up for residents.

I’m standing because I believe Chiswick Riverside wants something better: honest local leadership, practical action and representatives who will work hard for the community every day. My career has been spent in demanding public service and policy roles, helping deliver change and dealing seriously with complex challenges. I want to bring that same commitment, energy and focus to serving local people here in Chiswick Riverside.”

Rick’s selection comes as Chiswick Riverside emerges as one of the key battleground wards in Hounslow. PollCheck’s current ward-level projection lists the ward as a battleground, with Green ahead of Conservative by 2.2 percentage points, while its borough-wide projection suggests the Greens could make significant gains across Hounslow.

A spokesperson for Hounslow Green Party said:
“Rick Rowe is exactly the kind of candidate Chiswick Riverside needs. He combines serious professional experience with a strong commitment to fairness, sustainability and public service. Just as importantly, he lives in the ward and understands the community firsthand. Residents are looking for councillors who will show up, speak up and get things done, and Rick will be a strong voice for Chiswick Riverside.”

With the campaign now under way, Rick and local Green Party campaigners will be out across Chiswick Riverside meeting residents,

I am standing to represent Chiswick Riverside Ward because I believe we can build a fairer, more inclusive community - one where every resident feels heard, valued, and empowered.

As an active member of our borough, I am fully committed to serving the people of Chiswick Riverside with dedication, integrity, and energy.

My priorities are clear: supporting families, championing our local businesses, tackling inequality, improving public services and street safety, investing in young people, and fighting for genuinely affordable housing. Real change begins with listening. I am committed to turning residents’ concerns into meaningful action and practical solutions.

Professionally, I bring strong experience in eCommerce trading and retail mostly in the Fashion Industry. Skills that demand accountability, strategic thinking, and results. Beyond my professional work, my grassroots involvement reflects who I am. At my local Church, I serve on the Prayer Ministry, Welcoming Team, and Church Band, support the Alpha Course, and mentor women recovering from toxic relationships. I also help organise neighbourhood social events that create safe, welcoming spaces where people can connect and build community.

Service, community, and action are at the heart of everything I do. I am determined to work tirelessly for Chiswick Riverside. Campaigning on the doorstep, addressing local concerns, and being a visible, responsive, and effective representative who truly listens and delivers.

It would be an honour to earn your support.

Jessika

Muddassir Syed is a devoted family man, NHS employee, and active community member, standing as a Green Party candidate. He believes in leadership rooted in kindness, compassion, and hope, always putting the needs of the community above party politics.

Muddassir believes in strengthening the community to tackle issues such as fly-tipping, anti-social behaviour, and to better support families and older residents. He pledges to donate his councillor allowance to establish a Community Road Rescue – Cold Asphalt Fund to help fix potholes. He is also developing innovative ideas, including providing free food through vending machines and offering interest-free loans to help people avoid loan sharks.

Through his work in the NHS, Muddassir sees firsthand how challenges like air pollution, housing pressures, and inequality affect people’s daily lives. He brings this experience into his community efforts, addressing problems with practical solutions, collaboration, and care.

He is passionate about building a community where people feel connected, supported, and empowered. From supporting local businesses and protecting green spaces to improving transport and creating opportunities through education and skills initiatives, Muddassir is committed to making Chiswick Riverside a place where families can thrive and everyone can flourish.

As a Green Party candidate, Muddassir promises to listen, act with integrity, and stand up for fairness, sustainability, and meaningful change. He believes that through collaboration, compassion, and shared responsibility, the community can create a brighter, healthier, and more hopeful future for the next generation.

Muddassir also believes in accountability and is committed to engaging actively with residents, using technology and AI as tools to make everyday life easier and improve community wellbeing.

"Our area is incredibly special - sometimes I have to pinch myself that I get to call it home! The beautiful riverside, green spaces, indy businesses, and culture. I'll work hard for you to ensure we get what we need from the Council tomake the most of it."

I have lived in Chiswick since 2021. I am a senior policy advisor who has worked in the UK and European Parliaments, advising ministers, parliamentarians and officials on housing, small business and regeneration.

I founded and lead a national organisation driving research and advocacy for the UK's creative,cultural, and heritage sectors.

I studied at Cambridge later in life, am a Fellow of the Royal Society of Arts, and have published research and policy that has helped shape national legislation.

I have chaired several charities focussed on social mobility.

My Chiswick related articles can be seen here https://chiswick-labour.co.uk/blogs.php

Please visit www.chiswick-labour.co.uk to learn more about how I and the other members of the Labour team will keep Chiswick beautiful.

I have lived in Hounslow for over 30 years, where I raised my family and built strong local roots through decades of volunteering and community service.

After completing my MBA at the University of Liverpool, I have successfully garnered a senior leadership career in multinational IT companies.

I bring proven organisational and problem-solving skills, alongside a long-standing commitment to health, wellbeing and the environment, support for cancer fundraising, support for vunerable women, as well as hands-on work to improve local neighbourhoods and green spaces.

My council related articles can be seen here https://chiswick-labour.co.uk/blogs.php

Please visit www.chiswick-labour.co.uk to learn more about how I and the other members of the Labour team will keep Chiswick beautiful.

Frank is 57 years old and an eighth-generation West Londoner who has lived in Brentford, Acton and Chiswick. As a disabled person and a parent, he is passionate about pushing the local government to prioritise pavement maintenance and improve conditions for pedestrians, including those with pushchairs or mobility devices. He believes Riverside is a beautiful ward whose residents deserve better, and argues that the current Labour-run council treats the area as a cash cow, diverting resources elsewhere - something he says must change.

Nicolas is an engineer with a creative streak whose work spans the worlds of technology, architecture, and design. A dedicated Chiswick resident, he lives with his wife Natalie and their three children. His family fuels his passion for modernising education and driving innovation. Nicolas is running to bridge divides and bring people together, believing that when we work together we can make our world a better place, step by step.

James and his wife have lived in Chiswick for 20 years. He works in arts marketing, building audiences for plays and musicals in and around London. He also runs an incubator programme for new startups across the city. He has worked in the US, Germany and France and is a proud European. James is a keen cyclist - but he also loves his car and believes we need to find a healthy and sustainable balance for our neighbourhoods - to continue to make Chiswick more liveable for all.

Vilber has lived in Cranford for 10 years and is proud to call it home. He works at Heathrow Airport as a Customer Service Host and Workplace Union Representative, and is studying for a BA (Hons) in Global Business and Entrepreneurship. Having seen the pressures on local services, roads and small businesses, he is standing to be a councillor to bring his Heathrow experience and business skills to improving local services, supporting Cranford’s residents’ and ensuring that concerns are listened to and acted on.

Gurpreet has strong roots in Cranford and has been active in community work in and around the area for many years. He already works with local community groups to help keep Cranford clean and welcoming for everyone, and has led petitions on issues affecting local residents. Gurpreet has also served as a school governor, community first aider, mentor and In the little spare time he has, he enjoys hiking and recently led a team to complete the Three Peaks Challenge in 24 hours.

My name is Hira Dhillon, and I am standing to be the next local councillor for Cranford Ward following the sad passing of Cllr Sukhbir Dhaliwal.

Cranford is my home and it has been for 58 years. I have been involved in our community for a long time. I was a parent govenor of Cranford Junior School. I am currently a member of the Cranford Ward Panel, linking residents with the Police and Hounslow Council, and the Cranford Action Group which helps keep our streets clean.

I am retired now, but I spent most of my life working on the London Underground and railways as a guard, driver, and booking clerk.

It would be the honour of a lifetime to be chosen by my fellow residents of Cranford to represent them on Hounslow Council.

My pledges to you:

1. A local voice
I've lived here for nearly 60 years. I love Cranford and I will always put our community first.

2. Zero tolerance on antisocial behaviour
I'm already a member of the local Group which links residents with the Police and Hounslow Council. As your local Councillor I'll keep pushing police and the council to make Cranford safer.

3. State of the art Community Hub
Labour are building a new community centre and library at Meadowbank. I'll also be working with the Council to make sure that the current library site is used in a way which best suits Cranford.

4. Tough on flytipping and littering
Hounslow Labour have raised fines to the maximum legal limit, now we need to see them handed out to those who disrespect Cranford's streets and green spaces.

Prachi lives and also grew up in Cranford and is a proud member of the Cranford community. She studies Economics and has worked on local by-election campaigns and community projects. She is standing as a councillor candidate to help bridge the gap between Conservative values and real, everyday improvements for Cranford. Prachi is also focused on encouraging young people to get involved in local issues and on ensuring that residents’ concerns are listened to and acted on.

Gill Chapman, one of our campaign coordinators, joined the Green Party last summer at the start of the huge increase in members across the country, including in Hounslow. Now retired, she is standing for election in Cranford, having previously had a career as a university lecturer, followed by several years working in the charity sector. She says: “I have long supported the Green Party’s passion for the environment and concern for the very real threat of climate change, but I was inspired to join the party when they added a real concern about the inequality in our society to the mix. For too long we have allowed the wealthiest to continue to get richer still while others are struggling to make ends meet. Addressing this is fundamental to achieving the kind of compassionate and integrated society we all want.”

Renu has lived in Cranford for over 14 years - it is her home and her community. She understands the everyday issues residents face, from traffic and fly-tipping to GP access and the need for safer, cleaner streets. These are not abstract concerns, but part of daily life. She brings over 20 years of experience as a legal expert, mediator, and business leader, with a strong focus on resolving conflict, supporting communities, and standing up for fairness. Renu is standing in Cranford because the community deserves committed, visible, and effective representation.

These are our Reform UK Hounslow Policies:

Community Safety & Crime

Crime rates remain unacceptably high, and the council’s soft approach to law enforcement has failed to make a significant impact. Residents deserve to feel safe in their own neighbourhoods.We must tackle the increase in crime in Hounslow. Crime in the Borough has increased by 20% in the last 5 years alone.

Solutions:

- Enhanced Policing: Increase police presence in high-crime areas and adopt a zero-tolerance policy towards criminal behaviour. We must both invest in community policing initiatives to build trust and cooperation, whilst also cracking down on violent criminals with consistance & conviction. - Social Programmes: Whilst addressing the root causes of crime is important, immediate action is needed. Such as, Implementing targeted programmes to reduce youth unemployment, detatchment, depression, substance abuse & crime, but, we must not shy away from tough enforcement measures, especially when it comes to weapons & violent crime. - Tougher Sentencing: Advocate for mandatory minimum sentences for violent crimes and repeat offenders. This ensures that serious offenders are kept off the streets and sends a strong message of deterrence. - Stop and Search: Increase use of stop and search powers to tackle knife crime and other violent offences. Ensuring rigorous guidelines and accountability can balance effectiveness with community relations. - Front-Line Policing: Increase the number of front-line officers and enhance their visibility in local communities, such as a local liaison officers. This provides a deterrent against crime and ensures swift responses to incidents.

Prioritse Housing for Citizens and Working People:

The current system sometimes results in social housing going to those who do not contribute significantly to the community. Additionally, there are often long waiting lists for eligible citizens and working people.The primary goal of this policy is to prioritise the allocatio

I've been a resident of Hounslow for over a decade and observed the frustrating lack of care our wards are receiving.   As a councillor I would fight for more environmentally friendly policies, cleaner and safer streets, as well as more involvement of local community in the affairs of the ward.

Dan "The Bike Man" Barnett is wants to be your newest Councillor for Feltham North here are 3 reasons I'm the man for the job:
- You know where I am. Never before has a councillor made themselves available to the community 6 days a week, I will be. Find me at work on Baber Bridge Parade.
- I'm independent. No party political nonsense. No national agenda. Just doing the best for Feltham.
- I care about you. We deserve a safer, cleaner, nicer home. Let's make it happen

Vote for KRISSH Independent Councillor for FELTHAM NORTH (TW14)

A Community Leader, Dedicated to Serving You

As a pastor, teacher, mentor, and mother of 4 and grandmother of 5, I've lived the highs and lows of life. As a Dutch citizen who's worked hard to build a life in England, I understand the challenges families and communities face. Tamil native speaker, and other five languages like Sinhalese, Dutch, Malayalam, Hindi, Telugu.

I'm not a career politician. I'm a community champion, committed to listening and delivering for FELTHAM NORTH.

*My Commitment:*

- Supporting local families and businesses
- Improving community facilities and services
- Fostering a sense of community and belonging
- Providing a strong, independent voice

*My Experience:*

- Pastor/Teacher: Guiding and supporting individuals and families
- Mentor: Empowering young people to reach their potential
- Community Involvement: Active in local initiatives and organizations

*Let's Build a Better FELTHAM NORTH Together*

Vote KRISSH, Independent Councillor for FELTHAM NORTH

Contact: KRISSH21@hotmail.com
😊

My name is Nii Anum, and I am standing to represent you because Feltham deserves leadership that is present, accountable, and willing to act.

Let’s be honest, too many people feel overlooked. You see the same issues, rising costs, neglected streets, slow responses and nothing seems to change. That is not because solutions don’t exist. It is because the standard of leadership has been too low for too long.

I am not standing to manage decline. I am standing to raise the standard.

My focus is simple and non-negotiable:
- Cost of living: pushing back against unnecessary increases and demanding better financial decisions that actually protect residents
- Safer streets: dealing with issues early, not waiting until they escalate into bigger problems
- Stronger support for families: making services accessible, responsive, and accountable
- Cleaner environment: ensuring basic standards are met—rubbish cleared, streets maintained, problems dealt with properly
- Better local movement: improving transport, access, and everyday walkability

Let me be clear:
I will not make promises I cannot deliver
I will not disappear after elections
I will not accept poor performance as “normal”

I come from a background of building solutions: working in communities, designing interventions, and delivering real outcomes where it matters most. I understand what it takes to move from ideas to execution. And that is what is missing.

You don’t need another voice making noise.
You need leadership that produces results.

If elected, I will be visible, accountable and decisive.
This is a decision about the kind of leadership you want representing you.

If you want more of the same, nothing will change.
If you want better, then we need to choose differently.
This is about raising the standard for our streets, our families, and our future.

READ FULL MANIFESTO HERE: https://heyzine.com/flip-book/e341f28f0b.html

TACKLE THE COST OF LIVING
- Push back on excessive rent increases
 where the council has influence.

- Protect residents from being priced out of Feltham.

SAFER STREETS THROUGH
 EARLY INTERVENTION
- Support youth organisations.
- Spaces for young people locally.

REAL SUPPORT FOR FAMILIES 
- Cut waiting times for family support services
- Real provision for parents, carers and vulnerable households

Riza was born in Istanbul, Türkiye, in 1965. He went on to graduate with a degree in Electronics and Telecommunications Engineering, beginning his career with Turkish Telecom PLC. After moving to the UK in 1994, Riza continued to build on his expertise by gaining further qualifications in Electrical and Electronic Engineering, including ONC and HNC certifications. Over the years, he has worked with a range of leading lighting control companies, including Philips, Cooper, Eaton, Prolojik, and Leax, developing extensive experience within the industry. He currently works as an R&D Manager at TheisCraft Ltd, a company specialising in energy-saving and intelligent lighting control systems. Riza has lived in Feltham for over 30 years with his partner, Frances, and remains closely connected to the local community.

Simon has lived in Feltham for 30 years. Over the years he has been a local school governor and his daughter went to school locally. He has had a long career working in social housing with different communities across London and the South East and enjoys helping local groups bring their ideas together.

Lyndsay firmly believes it is their ethical responsibility to care for the environment, a principle that guides both their daily decisions and broader life choices in pursuit of a more meaningful life. They are motivated to support initiatives and policies that reduce waste, conserve resources, and promote sustainability and environmental awareness. Accordingly, Lyndsay strives to build healthier relationships and connect with people who share these values.

Edward has lived in the local area for 20 years. He lives with his wife and children. He works in the local community and also takes and active role in the community. He has a special interest in SEND support having co founded a family group with his wife owing to the lack of support available to families.

This is just a brief to say that I am the undisputed number 1 person for the job to fix anything and everything. The sole focus is local level and as a Feltham Resident, I want as many challenges, issues and obstacles thrown at me as physically possible to address each and every individual issues Heston Central has to offer. I make things happen and FAST. I work in Heston, so I am aware of the issues the town faces. People talk a good game, I play to win (Goalkeeper since 9 and 2x Powerleague Champion)

London born and raised with British Parents, ex MOD and British Army Reserve, National Mental Health first Aider, 15 Keyworker Badges and previously worked as a consultant with London Borough of Hounslow in Health and Social Care, Special Educational Needs and Street Cleansing. I know my stuff in this area so I WILL get through for the people.

Don't consider ANYONE else. No-one can get done what I will. Doubt me? I’ve put my Instagram and LinkedIn so you can DM me. I will answer EVERY question, query and critique. I will talk to anyone and will listen to EVERYTHING

I'm ALL IN. Bring it on!

Disclaimer: I am not racist (Indian/Kenyan Heritage), I am not a Zionist (I am 100% against ANY form of child abuse and have ZERO connections to Israel) and have zero wife or kids, therefore my focus and attention will not be diverted.

God Bless Us All 🙏

Dr. Nooralhaq Nasimi MBE has lived and worked in Hounslow for 12 years with and is the founder of the Afghanistan and Central Asian Association (ACAA), an award-winning Hounslow-based charity that has supported thousands of refugees and vulnerable families to rebuild their lives.

With decades of experience working directly with local communities, he understands the real challenges residents face, from access to services to community safety and integration.

Dr Nasimi is standing to strengthen local services, support families, and build a more inclusive and united Hounslow. He is committed to listening, taking action, and delivering real change for residents.

Josefina has lived in Hounslow for 17 years, who is currently self-employed.
She studied politics and international relations.

Chris is a local resident and business owner with 15+ years’ experience leading teams and delivering results. He understands pressures facing families and businesses. Active in the community, he supports initiatives and grassroots sport. Standing for Reform UK, Chris will focus on safer streets, cleaner neighbourhoods, tackling anti-social behaviour, and delivering practical improvements for residents across Heston and surrounding areas.

Rajesh Tewari was born in Isleworth and has lived his entire life in Hounslow Borough. He holds a first class honours degree in Criminology, Policing and Forensics, as well as a Master's degree with distinction in Criminology and Criminal Justice. He currently is employed by the Metropolitan Police Service with a career within Intelligence. Rajesh is standing for election in the Heston West Ward and if elected, shall focus on working with Hounslow police in helping to lower crime. His pledge is to work for the people in his ward in trying to secure more funds to achieve the objective of a safer ward.

Darius Nasimi has lived in Hounslow for 13 years, with his parents and 3 sisters and is a community campaigner and Head of Funding and Partnerships at ACAA, where he has helped secure funding and deliver programmes supporting refugees and local families across Hounslow.

A graduate of King’s College London, he founded a Central Asia initiative and has worked with organisations across the UK on community engagement and integration.

Growing up in a refugee family, Darius understands the challenges people face and is passionate about creating opportunities, supporting young people, and building stronger communities.

He is standing to ensure local voices are heard and to deliver real change for residents.

Mohan Bains is an experienced business leader and international advisor. He is a former President of the Asian Business Forum at the Thames Valley Chamber of Commerce in the UK and has served as an Adviser on European foreign investment to the Government of India, focusing on Haryana. He brings strong expertise in international trade, investment, and economic development.

I care deeply about the environment, and loss of biodiversity. I entered politics due to poor air pollution and the increasing concerns of the climate crisis.  I previously undertook pro bono legal work on the nuclear power consultation in 2009/2010 and did research on the nuclear industry, with the aim of requiring the government to agree to a Public Inquiry into future nuclear power contracts in the UK. I attended CND talks/campaigns on spreading information about the full details relating to the nuclear industry.

Green politics is the future. I will tirelessly campaign for the prioritisation of environmental needs over economic growth and push for my borough to seriously work towards reducing air pollution, and achieve cleaner air for residents, especially children. There should be restrictions implemented on traffic flow by schools, improvements in air quality throughout the borough. The Government is failing to protect its residents and children against respiratory and other diseases caused by air pollution, by road and air.  We desperately need change, and a proper roadmap in place to net zero. Our rivers, waterways and seas need to be cleaned up and protected. I promote a fair taxation system where corporations are proportionately responsible for pollution, we must stop taxation loopholes. We need money to be spent on the renewables industry and NHS. We have to fight for a world based on fairness and equality.

Ragveer Mattu is a dedicated community advocate and charity leader with more than 25 years of experience supporting disabled adults, vulnerable residents and low‑income families across London. Born in Hillingdon and raised in Ealing, Ragveer has lived in the
London Borough of Hounslow for over three decades, building deep local roots and a strong understanding of the community they serve.
Ragveer is currently studying at university to strengthen their understanding of legal, social
and policy issues. This ongoing learning reflects a long‑standing commitment to fairness, justice and ensuring that residents who are often overlooked receive the support they deserve. Ragveer’s political involvement began in 2010 when they stood as a Conservative candidate in the local elections. Since then, Ragveer has remained active in local politics,
working closely with councillors, party members and community organisations. They also use social media as a tool to highlight disability rights, raise awareness of local challenges and stay connected with residents across the borough. In the voluntary sector, Ragveer serves as Chairman of a charity supporting children with additional needs and their families. Through this work, they help provide essential guidance, practical support and reliable information to families navigating complex challenges. Ragveer also chairs a community food bank that distributes surplus supermarket food to disabled adults, elderly residents, low‑income households and
homeless individuals across London and Surrey. Operating seven days a week through a
dedicated team of volunteers, the service has become a vital lifeline for many.
Over the years, Ragveer has assisted thousands of residents facing difficulties related to housing, benefits, homelessness, council tax and financial hardship. Their hands‑on experience, compassion and commitment to public service continue to guide their mission: ensuring every resident is listened to, respected and supported.

Suresh has lived in Hounslow for 25 years with his wife and children. Over the years, he has developed a deep understanding of the community and the issues that matter most to local residents — from street safety and youth unemployment to the daily challenges faced by the elderly. He is standing for Councillor to be a true champion for the community, committed to listening, supporting, and delivering real change for everyone in Hounslow.

Having lived in Brentford for the last 12 years and born in the neighbouring borough; I have sound understanding and in-depth experience of West London and the local area. Working for many years in the fields of property maintenance, construction, facilities management, and safety compliance, and with personal experience of council accommodation, home ownership and the private rented sector, my knowledge of local housing and their amenities comes from grass roots. Moving to Brentford I found a great sense of community and really enjoy the local arts and music available. Needless to say, I have seen and lived through many changes to this part of London.

Teresa has lived in the area for over 20 years and understands the needs and concerns of the community. She is committed to tackling crime, improving local services, and ensuring the area remains a safe and welcoming place for everyone. If you care about your community and want real change, Teresa asks for your support.

Krishna has lived in Hounslow for 25 years and has been a resident of Hounslow South for the past 15 years, where he lives with his wife and children. He actively works with residents to address local issues such as illegal parking, fly-tipping, and road and pavement repairs, and has helped organise community street play events for children. Krishna understands the priorities of the local community and, if elected, will work hard to improve the area and ensure stronger accountability in local governance.

Syed has lived and worked in Hounslow for over 30 years and understands the daily challenges local families face. Standing for the Liberal Democrats, he is committed to tackling anti-social behaviour, improving street cleanliness, and driving ambitious town regeneration. He aims to be a councillor who truly listens to ensure the community remains a place residents can be proud of.

Miruna has lived in Hounslow South for five years and works in public affairs, while also helping out at a local pub. She is an active community volunteer, collecting donations for charities supporting animals and children. Miruna is passionate about helping residents, including members of the Eastern European community, understand their rights and get the support they need.

Carl Pierce is a software engineer and data analyst with 35 years of experience at British Airways. He has lived in Hounslow since 1987, having previously studied engineering at University of Cambridge. Alongside his main career, Carl also lectures in statistics for economics at University of West London. For Hounslow West, Carl is focused on improving street cleanliness, reducing traffic congestion, and delivering better parking solutions. He is concerned that selling off the car park at Hounslow West Station for additional housing could worsen overcrowding and strain local infrastructure. In his spare time, Carl enjoys chess, Dungeons & Dragons, tutoring maths, and spending time with his two cats.

I am a Labour councillor for Isleworth and Cabinet Member for Culture, Leisure and Public Spaces on Hounslow Council.

In 2022, I launched Grow for the Future, the UK’s first-ever policy to transform wasteland into allotments, community gardens and orchards and pair them with schools and community groups in deprived areas for people to grow food in the cost-of-living crisis and learn about sustainability, biodiversity and healthy living. The policy has been backed by the UK government and championed by Downton Abbey’s Jim Carter OBE. In 2026, I launched its new sister programme – Go Forth and Grow for the Future – as the first Right to Grow policy in West London and one of the first in the country, allowing residents to plant food and flowers on unused public land. The first two projects have seen a neglected alleyway in Isleworth brought to life with flowers planted by the community, and a beautiful new sensory garden on Ivybridge Estate.

In 2024, I partnered with Jamie Oliver to launch the celebrity chef’s first-ever food education programme directly targeted at primary schools to tackle childhood obesity. Kids from Ivybridge Primary School and across the borough have been taught how to cook healthy meals by the teachers, parents and community workers we’ve trained up.

Working closely with the community, I helped lead the successful campaign to save Park Road Allotments from being developed into flats by the Duke of Northumberland’s Estate. After planning permission was rejected, I led efforts to negotiate an agreement with Northumberland Estates, which has seen the council take on the lease of the land and secure the long-term future of the allotments.

I have consistently campaigned to prevent sewage dumping at Mogden, exposing Thames Water’s practices in the press and forcing the company to clean up after a major spill in Isleworth.

I’m working with Ealing Council, the GLA and the National Trust to build a new West London Regional Park, am bringing in new pa

Roger has lived in Isleworth for eight years and values the strong sense of community that makes the area a great place to live. After more than twenty years working as a solicitor specialising in mental capacity cases, he retrained as a teacher in 2022 and now works at a state secondary school in Hounslow. Engaging young people in local decision-making is important to him. He previously served as a Liberal Democrat councillor in Twickenham, helping introduce the pedestrianisation of Church Street and borough wide dementia strategy. Roger would work to empower residents and challenge the “Council knows best” approach in Hounslow.

It has privilege to represent Isleworth ward since 2022. I have lived in the Hounslow for almost 35 years, I believe that the only way for a councillor to represent their local community is for them to be part of the community and engage with the public to understand their issues, problems, hopes and aspirations. This involves more than simply attending surgeries waiting for issues to be presented, it means living in the community and actively engaging with the public, local agencies and community groups in order to build strong working relationships and develop a real sense of the issues in the wider borough and specifically within a ward. The only effective way to represent local residents is to be a resident, this allows for a free flow of views, ideas and issues in both directions. 
 I know that for the majority of people their interaction with government is at the local level and much of what affects their quality of life is also delivered at the local level, therefore I am determined that I will do everything possible to ensure that people get the quality of public service they deserve. Furthermore, I firmly believe everyone has the right to live and work in a safe, vibrant and pleasant environment and there is much local government can do to help build a better place in which to live and work. 
If re-elected I promise to do my very best to represent the people of Isleworth and ensure that everything I do is in the best interests of all our residents.

Rob has lived in Isleworth for 15 years and worked in the Borough of Hounslow for 23 years, giving him a strong understanding of local issues. His son attended a local school, and he served on a church council in Isleworth for 10 years, and many residents may recognise him from around the area walking his dog. Rob is standing to help break the status quo, ensure residents are properly represented, and hold the council and its decision-makers to account.

Judith has lived in Isleworth for 23 years and is a member of the board of a local residents' association. She holds a degree in Maths and previously worked as a systems programmer; she now works as an IT consultant. She is passionate about gender and social equality, tackling poverty, and protecting the environment. She actively campaigns to reduce fly-tipping and improve road safety in Isleworth. Together with her husband, she volunteers at Hounslow Community FoodBox and has also worked with several charities supporting people experiencing a crisis of homelessness. She believes strongly in transparency and honesty in politics and wants to help make Hounslow a clean, green and safe borough where the needs of residents always come first.

I am an experienced campaigner and have always supported improvements to cycling, walking and public transport, and the protection of open spaces. I joined with the " Save Our Rec " group of local residents who opposed plans to build a school on Brent Lea Recreation ground. I have my own allotment at Park Road in Isleworth and joined the successful campaign to stop the Duke of Northumberland building eighty flats on the allotments.   I provide care for elderly and vulnerable individuals. I enjoy singing and performs in local choirs and amateur dramatic groups. I live near the river in Isleworth.

I've lived in my current Isleworth address for the past 38 years.
My profession is Principal EEE Parts Project Engineer at Airbus Defence & Space. I support all project-level activities for Science, Military and Telecoms Satellite programmes. I was nvolved in the ExoMars rover exploration mission program led by the European Space Agency (ESA) in partnership with NASA, dedicated to searching for signs of past or present life on Mars.
My education includes two master's degrees from King's College, London University and the Communications Research Centre at Lancaster University.

As a Hounslow resident of over a decade, I am an Educator, UCL Education Specialist, and Property Expert with a decade of experience in the housing sector. I combine this practical expertise with the analytical rigour of my MSc in Global Business Management (University of Roehampton) and BSc in Physics to advocate for higher standards in Isleworth. My approach to local governance is rooted in transparent, SMART [(S)pecific, (M)easurable, (A)chievable, (R)elevant, (T)ime-bound] accountability. By championing STEM skills and data-driven public services, I work to ensure our residents' needs remain the focus of every decision.

Sapphire was born in Camden, London, raised in the Borough of Brent, and has lived in Hounslow for nearly 40 years, with deep and long‑standing roots in the borough. Their children were born and raised locally, giving Sapphire a strong personal connection to the community and a clear understanding of the realities faced by local families.

Sapphire is the founder and trustee of a registered charity supporting disabled children and their families. Informed by lived experience—with Sapphire’s own child being the inspiration behind the charity’s formation—this work is grounded in a first‑hand understanding of the challenges families face when navigating services, accessing support, and understanding rights and entitlements. The charity’s work focuses on reducing barriers, improving access, and ensuring families are supported in a fair, informed, and respectful way.

In addition, Sapphire is the founder and trustee of a foodbank supporting elderly people, individuals experiencing homelessness, people with disabilities, and low‑income families. Through this role, Sapphire has helped deliver practical support to some of the borough’s most vulnerable residents, while ensuring services operate within appropriate safeguarding, regulatory, and governance frameworks. This work has strengthened local partnerships and contributed to more consistent and accountable community support.

Alongside charitable responsibilities, Sapphire is currently studying at university, developing advanced knowledge of legal frameworks, policy interpretation, and structured decision‑making. This knowledge supports a more informed, rights‑based approach to community work, helping local people better understand processes, challenge barriers appropriately, and access services and support to which they are entitled.

Together, this combination of long‑term local knowledge, lived experience, and legally informed community leadership contributes to positive outcomes for residents. It supports stronger ad

Shane was born and raised in Hounslow and has lived in the borough for over 30 years, giving Shane a strong and enduring connection to the local community and a well‑grounded understanding of the issues affecting residents and families across the area.

Shane serves as a trustee for a charity supporting disabled children and their families, as well as providing essential support to individuals experiencing homelessness. Through this role, Shane contributes to effective governance, oversight, and responsible decision‑making, helping to ensure services are sustainable, accountable, and focused on delivering meaningful support to those most in need.

Alongside charitable responsibilities, Shane is at university, developing professional knowledge and skills related to financial oversight, budgeting, risk management, and organisational accountability. These skills support a structured and evidence‑based approach to community work, with a strong focus on transparency, value for money, and the responsible use of resources.

This combination of long‑term local roots, trustee experience, and financially informed decision‑making contributes to stronger governance and more resilient services within the community. It supports better planning, clearer accountability, and improved outcomes for local people in Hounslow, now and moving forward.

I am a qualified accountant and have lived in the borough for nearly 30 years. I want an end to wastefulness and for resources to be directed to cleaning up our streets, tackling crime, and caring for the young and elderly. We need value for our money and more openness and accountability. I would like to inspire members of the public to help elderly people and, in particular, to end their isolation and reintegrate them with the community, bringing happiness and joy to the lives of people who need care and attention.

Tom Fidler is a local resident with deep roots in the borough, where his family has lived and worked for generations, and he previously served as a councillor in neighbouring Spelthorne. During his time there, he played a key role in a cross-party coalition that ended long-standing one-party control and helped restore balance and accountability to local government, while also championing financial responsibility and sustainable investment. Outside politics, Tom supports local community life as a Brentford season ticket holder and grassroots sports supporter, and as Chair of Spelthorne and Middlesex Heritage he successfully campaigned to protect the area’s identity, helping secure Middlesex heritage signs in Brentford.

Millicent is standing for election in Hounslow, the community where she has lived and raised her children for the past eight years. Deeply involved in local schools, she is committed to creating a better future for her neighbours by seizing the opportunities presented by ongoing local changes. Her key priorities include lowering energy costs through efficiency and protecting the biodiversity of the area's vital green spaces.

Over the past 10 years, Christopher has lived, worked and brought his family up in this borough. He loves and has always felt strongly connected to the amazing community that makes up this ward and believes it deserves the opportunity of real choice on election day. At home, these values are often reinforced by his five-year-old son, Eddie, who likes to remind everyone of the “golden rules” they follow as a family: be nice to people, try your best, and be careful.
Address: 42 Avenue Road, TW8 9NS Contact me: ‭+44 7950 468361‬

Lionel has lived in Brentford all his life and has a degree in Leisure management and is now retired. He has supported Brentford football club for nearly 60 years and has also been a county member of Middlesex CCC for over 40 years. He would like to see Hounslow and Brentford in particular continue to become a thriving town whilst keeping its very historical heritage.

As a long term resident of Chiswick and Brentford with 3 generations of her family living here, Phyllis is very conscious of the importance of our community to residents. She advocates for clean streets, green areas and play areas for young and old, aswell as social support, education and health when we need it. Phyllis volunteers at the West Middlesex hospital and the Musical Museum. As a Liberal Democrat, she believes in our values of equality of opportunity for all and value the diversity of life in Hounslow. Our core values are: liberty, equality, democracy, community, human rights, internationalism and environmentalism.
